title: Aurinia cyclocarpa
intro: Aurinia cyclocarpa, commonly known as the prickly candytuft, is a perennial plant species in the Brassicaceae family, known for its distinctive clusters of small, white flowers and prickly foliage. The health benefits of Aurinia cyclocarpa include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aurinia cyclocarpa is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical ointment to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Aurinia cyclocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Aurinia, which is a small group of plants native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Aurinia cyclocarpa is further categorized as a perennial herb, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The species is often found growing in alpine meadows and rocky slopes, where it thrives in well-drained soil and full sun. Its classification is further supported by its botanical name, which includes the specific epithet 'cyclocarpa', meaning 'hollow-fruited'.

properties: Aurinia cyclocarpa, also known as the needle spurge or needle spurge thistle, has been traditionally used in medicine.
